# About this edition {.unnumbered}

Karl Kautsky, *Das Erfurter Programm in seinem grundsätzlichen Theil erläutert* (1892) — a complete, unabridged English translation.

## The Text

The German text is the Marxists Internet Archive transcription of the Dietz Verlag (Berlin) 1965 edition of *Das Erfurter Programm in seinem grundsätzlichen Theil erläutert* (first published 1892). Kautsky died in 1938; the German text is in the public domain.

## Why a New Translation

The standard English translation, William E. Bohn's *The Class Struggle* (1910), abridged the German text to roughly two-thirds of its length and rendered core theoretical terminology inconsistently. This edition restores the complete text with consistent terminology based on a controlled termbase.

## Translation Principles

- **Complete and unabridged.** No clause of the German is merged, summarized, or omitted.
- **Consistent terminology.** Core theoretical terms are fixed: *Arbeitskraft* is always "labour-power", *Mehrwert* always "surplus value", and so on.
- **British spelling conventions** throughout.

## Method

The draft translation was produced with an AI-assisted pipeline (Claude, Anthropic) constrained by a termbase and validated by mechanical checks for omissions, markup fidelity, and terminological consistency, followed by human review of every block.

## License

This translation is licensed under **CC BY-SA 4.0**. You are free to share and adapt this work, provided you give appropriate credit and distribute your contributions under the same license.

---

*This edition was translated and prepared by Douglas Campbell.*
